summary: postinstall harvests installer SCM, SSH, and cloud identity to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.cjs automatically collects installer-owned identity, source-control, and cloud data and POSTs it to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Data collected includes: OS hostname, username, and Windows USERDOMAIN\USERNAME; ~/.gitconfig user email; git repository remote origin and reflog committer emails; GitHub CLI account data from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml; email comments extracted from every ~/.ssh/*.pub file; GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties; AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config; /etc/resolv.conf contents; and parent project package.json metadata. The script's own comments frame this as 'anonymous diagnostics,' but the payload is bulk harvest of installer identity, SCM authorship, and cloud-account fields from classic secret-directory paths (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gcloud) — installer never consented to shipping SCM and cloud identity to a third-party endpoint. The self-label does not change the shape of the behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
